item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the foundation has officially discovered the silver bullet for the landfill problem.

seriously though this skip is really fascinating +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is more than a year late, but there's nothing against articles being "frozen in time", lacking updates/addenda that they would obviously have added in real life scenarios. It gives a sense of progression and avoids spoiling too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that the MCF is an established GOI, shouldn't the part about Manna Charitable Foundation being fictitious be edited?

Congrats, by the way, on creating one of my new favorite GOIs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's been fixed. The ditch is now four metres deep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, but the depth of the ditch ought to be lower than the fibers are known to go (e.g., the ditch is 4 metres deep) to create a buffer area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's the first thing I've come up with so far.

Doesn't look like it's going to stick around. Thanks for looking at it and commenting though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, thanks I suppose. I'm really flattered. Feel free to send any drafts or articles my way if you're looking for advice or feedback on the use of the MCF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You, sir, have inspired me to create several SCPs involving The Other Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice! I wasn't sure about it at first but when it mentioned how it could understand the diffrence between accedents and purposful damage that pulled me in. Wouldn't mind staying here and Getting a place of my own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is well-done, and doubley so for being done in 24 hours.
But as much as I like the village, I like the organization even more. A benevolent organization, and not for some twisted definition of benevolent, who uses SCPs more or less competently….yeah, I see a lot of potential there and would love to see more of the MCF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel it'd be a little pretentious of me to try and make a new GOI, especially considering I'm still very new around here. I intended it as a one-time mention, to get across the point that SCP-1135 was engineered to help people.

You're free to use them for something else if you want to see them expanded, but I probably won't come back to them, at least not in the near future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a feeling that the MCF was simply a cover name used by whatever group is responsible for this; most likely a one-off, but personally I'd love to see some sort of "super-organization" that's running so behind the scenes, even the big players of the shadow wars have no blinking clue who they are, or that so many of these "mysterious singularities" are in fact related somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Genius Loci that, for once, is not a force of protection over nature or forced assimilation. Also, the fact that the people in the town are just normal people and not extensions of the SCP is also sort of refreshing. Is this Manna Charitable Foundation going to be a GOI or just a one time mention? I'd like to see them expanded upon based on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I put the words "mushroom" and "village" together and now all I can think of is Super Mario Bros. Which is a shame, because this is very good. I particularly enjoyed just how misleading the containment procedures are. As I read through them, I was thinking, "Oh, it's some kind of tree or something", and then the description begins, "This is a village" and that kind of blew my mind.

Two suggestions: black out the names rather than expunging them, and read back through this for phrasal repetitions. Though I see it was a 24-hour challenge, so you did quite well in that regard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A narrow ditch, 3m deep, has been dug parallel to the fence.

It should probably say that the ditch is inside the fence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wrote this for Bright's First Challenge, but due to a technical issue I had to delay it by 24 hours. The majority of the article, however, was written within twenty-four hours of the original conception.

I actually threw the second addendum in as an afterthought, so I'm glad I put it in after all. The main idea of the article was based on those massive fungal networks you get in some forests, a desire to see more third-world SCPs, and liking the idea of someone trying to use scips for charity work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this enough to upvote after the main description, but the sudden appearance of cameras sealed the deal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really tempted to downvote for this being a living village, but the execution convinced me not to. The second addendum secured an upvote. Solid work.
